headline: Buffett Hands Reins To Abel As Berkshire Revives Buybacks And Ownership Focus
summary: Warren Buffett has retired as CEO of Berkshire Hathaway (NYSE:BRK.A) after roughly six decades at the company. Greg Abel has taken over as CEO, marking a formal handover of day to day leadership. Abel has made a significant personal purchase of Berkshire shares, aligning his financial stake more closely with shareholders. Berkshire has resumed share repurchases after a pause, signaling a renewed use of buybacks in its capital allocation toolkit. Berkshire Hathaway operates across multiple...

headline: Greg Abel Signals Berkshire Confidence With Personal Buy And Buybacks
summary: New CEO Greg Abel purchased US$15 million in NYSE:BRK.B stock, signaling personal commitment to the company. Berkshire Hathaway resumed share buybacks for the first time in nearly two years under Abel's leadership. These are among the first major capital allocation moves since Warren Buffett's retirement. Berkshire Hathaway, the conglomerate behind businesses in insurance, energy, railroads and consumer brands, is entering a new chapter with Greg Abel as CEO. His personal US$15 million...
